title: euclea crispa subsp. ovata
intro: Euclea crispa subsp. ovata, also known as the 'Buchu', is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the family Ebenaceae and is native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in treating urinary tract infections and other related issues. The health benefits of Euclea crispa subsp. ovata include its ability to relieve kidney stones, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of prostatitis. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, by drying and grinding its leaves to create a powder for medicinal purposes.
